title: 🔍 AWS IoT Jobs Explorer (Boto3)
learning_description:
This script helps you explore and understand AWS IoT Jobs in detail. You'll learn how
to inspect job configurations, monitor job executions across devices, and understand
job status reporting. This is really helpful for managing OTA deployments and troubleshooting
device update issues in production IoT environments.

warnings.debug_warning: ⚠️  WARNING: Debug mode exposes sensitive information (ARNs, account IDs, API responses)

prompts.debug_mode: 🔧 Enable debug mode (show all API calls and responses)? [y/N]:
prompts.enter_job_id_executions: 📝 Enter Job ID to explore executions:
prompts.continue_exploration: Would you like to explore more? [Y/n]:

status.scanning_jobs: 🔍 Scanning for IoT jobs across all statuses...
status.getting_execution_details: 🔍 Getting job execution details for device: {}
status.exploration_info: 💡 You can use this information to understand your IoT deployment status and troubleshoot issues.

errors.invalid_number: ❌ Please try entering a valid number
errors.both_required: ❌ Both Job ID and Thing Name are required
errors.failed_job_details: ❌ Couldn't get job details. The job might not exist.
errors.failed_execution_details: ❌ Couldn't get job execution details. Please check the job ID and thing name.

ui.explore_job_execution: 3. Explore job execution by device and job ID
ui.list_job_executions: 4. List job executions for a specific job

table_headers.executions: Thing Name                Status       Execution #  Queued At

ui.cancelled: 👋 Job exploration cancelled by user. Goodbye!

results.no_jobs_found: ℹ️  No jobs found in your account
results.no_executions_found: ℹ️  No job executions found for job: {}
results.no_active_jobs: ℹ️  No active jobs to cancel

learning.job_status_tip: 💡 Learning Tip: Job statuses indicate the overall progress of your OTA deployment.
learning.in_progress_tip: IN_PROGRESS jobs are actively deploying to devices.
learning.job_document_tip: 💡 Learning Tip: The job document contains the instructions sent to devices.
learning.presigned_url_tip: Presigned URLs allow secure S3 access without device credentials.
learning.execution_status_tip: 💡 Learning Tip: Job executions track individual device progress.
learning.status_details_tip: Status details contain device-reported information about success/failure.
learning.execution_progress_tip: 💡 Learning Tip: Job execution status shows how your OTA deployment is progressing.
learning.succeeded_tip: SUCCEEDED means the device successfully completed the update.
learning.api_access_note: 💡 Note: This script can now access job execution details for jobs in any status (including COMPLETED, FAILED, etc.)
learning.cancel_tip_1: 💡 Tip: Use option 2 to view the canceled job details
learning.cancel_tip_2: 💡 Tip: Use option 6 to delete the canceled job when ready
learning.delete_tip: 💡 Tip: The job will no longer appear in job lists
learning.delete_tip_force: 💡 Try again with force flag if job has executions
learning.delete_tip_cancel_first: 💡 Tip: Only COMPLETED or CANCELED jobs can be deleted
learning.delete_tip_use_cancel: 💡 Tip: Use option 5 to cancel IN_PROGRESS jobs first

cancel.warning: ⚠️  WARNING: This action cannot be undone!
cancel.when_cancel_1: • QUEUED executions will NOT start (prevented from beginning)
cancel.when_cancel_2: • IN_PROGRESS executions will complete their current operation (they'll finish what they're doing)
cancel.when_cancel_3: • Job status changes to CANCELED (visible in job lists)
cancel.when_cancel_4: • You can still view job history and execution details
cancel.when_cancel_5: • You can delete canceled jobs later if you want to clean up
cancel.why_cancel_1: • Canceling keeps the execution history so you can analyze what happened
cancel.why_cancel_2: • It's helpful for troubleshooting why a job needed to stop
cancel.why_cancel_3: • You can review what succeeded before you canceled it
cancel.scenario_1: • Firmware update causing device issues → Cancel to prevent more failures
cancel.scenario_2: • Wrong configuration deployed → Cancel and create corrected job
cancel.scenario_3: • Testing job that should not reach production → Cancel before it spreads

delete.warning: ⚠️  WARNING: This will permanently delete the job and all execution history!
delete.deletion_removes_header: Job deletion permanently removes the job record from AWS IoT:
delete.deletion_removes_1: • Job configuration and document are deleted
delete.deletion_removes_2: • ALL execution history is permanently deleted
delete.deletion_removes_3: • This action can't be undone - there's no way to recover it
delete.deletion_removes_4: • You'll need the 'force' flag if the job has any execution history
delete.when_delete_1: • Cancel: Stops the job but keeps history so you can analyze it
delete.when_delete_2: • Delete: Removes completed or canceled jobs to keep things tidy
delete.when_delete_3: • Best practice: Cancel first, take a look at what happened, then delete
delete.why_force_1: • AWS protects you against accidentally losing data
delete.why_force_2: • The force flag confirms you understand the execution history will be lost
delete.why_force_3: • Jobs without executions can be deleted without needing the force flag
delete.scenario_1: • Test jobs after validation → Delete them to keep your job list clean
delete.scenario_2: • Old completed jobs → Delete them after you've saved the results elsewhere
delete.scenario_3: • Canceled jobs after analysis → Delete once you've documented findings
delete.force_required: ⚠️  Force deletion required (job has execution history)

statistics.health_excellent: ✅ Excellent - Job is performing very well
statistics.health_good: ⚠️  Good - Some failures detected, keep an eye on it
statistics.health_poor: ❌ Poor - High failure rate, worth investigating what's going on
statistics.state_queued: • QUEUED: Job received but hasn't started on the device yet
statistics.state_in_progress: • IN_PROGRESS: Device is actively executing the job
statistics.state_succeeded: • SUCCEEDED: Job completed successfully on device
statistics.state_failed: • FAILED: Job execution failed (worth checking the device logs)
statistics.state_rejected: • REJECTED: Device rejected the job (could be a version mismatch, etc.)
statistics.success_95_100: • 95-100%: Excellent - Job is working as expected
statistics.success_80_94: • 80-94%: Good - Minor issues, acceptable for most scenarios
statistics.success_50_79: • 50-79%: Poor - There are significant issues that need looking into
statistics.success_below_50: • <50%: Critical - The job likely has some fundamental problems
statistics.pattern_timed_out: • High TIMED_OUT: Try increasing the job timeout or optimizing the job document
statistics.pattern_rejected: • High REJECTED: Worth checking device software versions and compatibility
statistics.pattern_failed: • High FAILED: Worth reviewing the job document syntax and device capabilities
statistics.practice_1: • Keep an eye on statistics during rollout to catch issues early
statistics.practice_2: • Use SNAPSHOT target selection for gradual rollouts
statistics.practice_3: • Consider canceling jobs with >20% failure rate to prevent fleet-wide issues
statistics.rec_no_executions: • No executions found - the job may not have started yet
statistics.rec_check_devices: • Worth checking if target devices are online and connected
statistics.rec_verify_groups: • Worth verifying thing group membership if you're using group targets
statistics.rec_canceled_early: • The job was canceled before any executions completed
statistics.rec_review_cancel: • Worth reviewing the cancellation reason to understand why it was stopped
statistics.rec_view_details: • Use option 2 to view full job details and history
statistics.rec_devices_removed: • All executions were removed (the devices may have been deleted)
statistics.rec_verify_exist: • Worth verifying the target devices still exist in the registry
statistics.rec_in_progress: • The job is still in progress - no completed executions yet
statistics.rec_wait: • Worth waiting for more executions to complete before analyzing
statistics.rec_monitor: • Keep an eye on progress and check back later for updated statistics
statistics.rec_high_failure: • High failure rate detected - worth taking immediate action
statistics.rec_investigate: • Try using option 3 to investigate specific device failures
statistics.rec_check_logs: • Worth checking device logs for error details
statistics.rec_consider_cancel: • You might want to consider canceling the job to prevent more failures (option 5)
statistics.rec_monitor_closely: • Keep a close eye on it as the job continues
statistics.rec_continue_monitor: • Keep monitoring for any changes in success rate
statistics.rec_document: • Worth documenting successful patterns for future jobs
statistics.rec_good: • Job is performing well with minor issues
statistics.rec_review_failed: • Worth reviewing failed executions to identify patterns
statistics.rec_acceptable: • Think about whether the failure rate is acceptable for your use case
statistics.rec_mixed: • The job has completed with mixed results
statistics.rec_understand_patterns: • Worth reviewing execution details to understand failure patterns
